pattern: A perfect pairing by equal values exists exactly when every distinct value occurs an even number of times, so a single frequency tally settles the question.
hints: Pairs only ever match two copies of the SAME value, so the only thing that matters is how many times each value occurs.
If any value occurs an odd number of times, at least one copy of it can never find a partner.
Count occurrences of every value; the array can be divided into equal pairs if and only if every count is even.

edge_inputs.description: a value that occurs an odd number of times among otherwise-even counts
no repeats at all, cannot pair anything
minimum length, a single matching pair
one value appears four times (even) so it must not be flagged as unpairable
all identical values, always pairs cleanly regardless of count parity of the array length

stress.description: 500 values, always constructed with every value appearing an even number of times so the answer is deterministically true
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    pairs = max(1, size // 2)
    nums = []
    for _ in range(pairs):
        v = rng.randrange(1, 501)
        nums.extend([v, v])
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums]
stress.note: constructed as pairs so the answer is always true; correctness for the false branch is covered by the edge/trap cases
